taskDescription: Arrange the sequence of structures encountered when tracing portal blood flow from intestine to liver (AN47.8, AN47.10).
paragraphs: Venous blood from the intestines drains into the superior mesenteric vein (small intestine, caecum, ascending and transverse colon)
Venous blood from the spleen, stomach, and pancreas drains into the splenic vein
The superior mesenteric vein and splenic vein unite behind the neck of the pancreas to form the portal vein
The portal vein ascends in the free edge of the lesser omentum (hepatoduodenal ligament) with the hepatic artery and bile duct
The portal vein enters the liver at the porta hepatis and divides into right and left branches supplying the hepatic sinusoids
